vulnerability.title: Ensure remote access methods are monitored in Rsyslog
vulnerability.description: Logging of remote access methods must be implemented to help identify cyber attacks and ensure ongoing compliance with remote access policies are being audited and upheld. An examples of a remote access method is the use of the Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) from an external, non-organization controlled network. The /etc/rsyslog.conf or /etc/rsyslog.d/*.conf file should contain a match for the following selectors: auth.*, authpriv.*, and daemon.*. If not, use the following as an example configuration: auth.*;authpriv.* /var/log/secure daemon.* /var/log/messages
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && rpm --quiet -q rsyslog; then declare -A REMOTE_METHODS=( ['auth.*']='^[^#]*auth\.\*.*$' ['authpriv.*']='^[^#]*authpriv\.\*.*$' ['daemon.*']='^[^#]*daemon\.\*.*$' ) declare -A LOCATIONS=( ['auth.*']='/var/log/secure' ['authpriv.*']='/var/log/secure' ['daemon.*']='/var/log/messages' ) if [[ ! -f /etc/rsyslog.conf ]]; then # Something is not right, create the file touch /etc/rsyslog.conf fi # Loop through the remote methods associative array for K in "${!REMOTE_METHODS[@]}" do # Check to see if selector/value exists if ! grep -rq "${REMOTE_METHODS[$K]}" /etc/rsyslog.*; then APPEND_LINE=$(sed -rn "/^\S+\s+\${LOCATIONS[$K]}$/p" /etc/rsyslog.conf) # Make sure we have a line to insert after, otherwise append to

triage_decision.reason: Adding or ensuring rsyslog selectors for auth.*, authpriv.*, and daemon.* is a standard configuration change that does not affect authentication mechanisms or system availability. It simply enhances logging and carries no risk of lockout or service disruption.

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to produce a Plan describing changes. Then call review_plan. After approval proceed. We need to modify rsyslog config lines. Likely file /etc/rsyslog.conf or maybe /etc/rsyslog.d/remote-access.conf. We need to ensure selectors for auth.*, authpriv.*, daemon.*. So plan: check /etc/rsyslog.conf; if selectors missing, add. Ensure no duplicates. Use sed to uncomment if commented. Use run_cmd with sed to modify lines. Use read_file to preview. Must not chain. Ok.

Plan description: We'll get current config. Use grep to see lines. If auth.* etc missing, then append lines. Use sed -i to uncomment commented lines. Use echo >> to append lines. Use minimal changes. We'll use /etc/rsyslog.conf. The plan: read /etc/rsyslog.conf, search for patterns. For each selector, if present (case-insensitive?) then uncomment if commented. If not present, append at end of file. We'll use sed to replace '# *auth.*' etc, but need safe. Use regex: if line starts with optional spaces then '#', then any spaces, auth.*; for each. Then replace to remove '#'. Also if full line missing, then use echo. Also ensure trailing newline. After changes, run 'systemctl restart rsyslog' to apply.

We need to use one command per call. So plan.

But must call review_plan first. Then after approval, create step by step.

Proceed.

We need to provide a plan using review_plan first. The user provided a finding requiring adding match lines for auth.*, authpriv.*, and daemon.* to /etc/rsyslog.conf or any file in /etc/rsyslog.d. We cannot just write arbitrary lines; we need to check existing config. We should check /etc/rsyslog.conf and see if there are lines matching those selectors. If missing, add lines.

We should not modify sshd or other config. So we plan: read /etc/rsyslog.conf. Search for lines matching ^\s*auth\.\* or ^\s*authpriv\. or ^\s*daemon\. (Note that in config syntax it's 'auth.*;authpriv.* /var/log/secure' etc). We should check both.

If missing, add appropriate lines at the end of file.

We should use sed -i for in-place modifications. But here we only need to add lines; not modify.

Thus plan: read_file /etc/rsyslog.conf. Run grep to check each selector. For missing ones, use run_cmd to append to file (cat <<EOF >>). But we must not chain commands. But we can have separate run_cmd appended each.

We can use "echo 'auth.*;authpriv.* /var/log/secure' >> /etc/rsyslog.conf" etc.

But we need to add lines individually. But if we need to check and then conditionally append? We can just append all three lines at end but could duplicate lines if already exist; need to avoid duplicates. Since we read file and check presence, we can store missing selectors in script. But system commands must be single commands. Could do multiple run_cmd. There's no flow in tool usage; we need to chain logic in our mind. So we must read file first, then perform logic to decide. We can do 3 separate checks: one for auth.*, one for authpriv.*, one for daemon.*. For each missing, run echo to append.

We should not chain all into one command; each run_cmd will apply. So the plan: read_file conf. Check for selector presence via grep. Then for each missing, run_cmd to append. That is fine.

But we need to include plan in JSON via review_plan. Then we will do feedback.

Thus the plan must be in plan_description string. We'll describe reading, checking, appending.

Let's craft plan.
